description: Stable RoP-internal identifier for a curated CDE collection. Format: 'RoP-Collection:NNNNNNN'. A collection is a project-specific, curator-blessed bundle of CDEs drawn from one or more upstream source authorities, plus DataTecnica-derived additions where standard authorities don't cover the project's needs. Examples: Path-ND, GP2-CDE, CARD-Catalog-CDE, Gates-Ventures-CDE, ADSP-PHC-CDE. Each version of a collection is a distinct CollectionID.

metadata_.stability: never recycled; deprecated collections retain ID with is_active=false

description: Full human-readable collection name. Examples: 'Path-ND Common Data Elements', 'GP2 Phenotypic Data Standards', 'CARD Catalog Curated Resources', 'Gates Ventures Neurodegeneration CDE Pack', 'ADSP-PHC Harmonized Phenotype Set'.

Short identifier used as the value in `member_of_collections` arrays on individual CDE rows. Examples: Path-ND, GP2, CARD, GatesV, ADSP-PHC. This is the join key — every CDE row tagged `member_of_collections=['Path-ND']` resolves through this abbreviation.

Version identifier following semantic versioning where applicable. Collections evolve as new CDEs are added, deprecated CDEs are retired, and value-set memberships shift. Pin to a specific version when citing a collection in published methods.
Free-text description of the collection's purpose, scope, and intended use. One paragraph recommended.

Organization that owns and maintains the collection. JSON object with name, identifier (ROR or DUNS), contact, and governance URL. Examples: NIH CARD, GP2 Steering Committee, DataTecnica LLC, Gates Ventures.
Curators responsible for the collection. JSON array of {name, orcid, affiliation, role} entries. Role distinguishes lead-curator, contributing-curator, technical-reviewer, scientific-reviewer.

What the collection covers. JSON object with disease areas, modalities covered, target cohorts, and explicit out-of-scope items. Helps downstream consumers decide whether the collection is appropriate for their use case.

schema.disease_areas: JSON array of Mondo/HPO disease codes (e.g., ['Mondo:0005180', 'Mondo:0007089'])
schema.modalities: JSON array of modality tags from Module 12 ResourceCoarseModality vocabulary
schema.target_cohorts: JSON array of cohort/resource names this collection is designed for
schema.out_of_scope: Free-text description of what the collection deliberately does not cover

description: Upstream source authorities that contribute CDEs to this collection. Most curated collections draw from multiple authorities (Path-ND uses NACC, NINDS-CDE, LOINC, HPO, plus DataTecnica-derived rows for gaps). JSON array of source authority names.

metadata_.rationale: Tracks provenance at the collection level so downstream consumers know what licensing and update cycles apply. A collection drawing from NACC (DUA-restricted) and LOINC (open) inherits the more restrictive of the two for any compound use.

description: When this collection is derived from or extends another collection, the parent CollectionID values. JSON array. Example: Path-ND-Genetic-Subset is derived from Path-ND. Enables provenance traversal and avoids duplicating shared content.

Other collections that cover similar scope but are independently curated. Examples: Path-ND ≈ NACC UDS-3.0 in cognitive coverage; GP2-CDE ≈ MJFF-PD-CDE in PD-specific coverage. JSON array of {collection_id, overlap_estimate, scope_diff_summary}. Supports cross-collection harmonization decisions.

schema_per_entry.overlap_estimate: Approximate fraction of CDEs in common (0.0-1.0)

description: Collection-level governance summary. JSON object with default consent class (Module 9 DUO terms), default sharing posture, and DUA reference. Most CDEs in a collection inherit these defaults; per-CDE overrides go in Module 9.

schema.default_duo_class: GRU | HMB | DS | POA | GSO | NPU | NRES | Mixed | Unknown
schema.default_download_permitted: permitted | permitted-with-DAC-approval | summary-statistics-only | model-weights-only | prohibited | unknown
schema.dua_reference: DOI or URL of governing DUA
schema.license: SPDX identifier or descriptive license name

description: 0-9 FAIR score for the collection itself (carries CARD methodology to the collection level). Distinct from per-resource FAIR scores in Module 12 — a collection of FAIR-compliant resources can still score poorly on its own meta-FAIR if the collection lacks versioning, persistent identifiers, or documentation.

Snapshot of how many CDE rows belong to this collection. JSON object {total, by_module, by_source_authority, snapshot_date}. Enables size-based prioritization in the UI and tracks growth over time. Computed at bundle build time, not maintained by hand.

schema.snapshot_date: ISO date the count was computed

description: Module 12 ResourceID values that produce or consume this collection's CDEs. A collection like Path-ND links to its source resources (NACC, GP2, PPMI, ADSP-PHC). JSON array.

Tracks the upstream-submission lifecycle for DataTecnica-derived CDEs in this collection. Many boutique CDEs fill gaps that standard authorities don't cover; the long-term goal is to submit those gaps upstream (to LOINC, HPO, etc.) for permanent inclusion. This field tracks where each candidate is in that pipeline.

metadata_.rationale: Promotion to upstream authorities is the right end-state for genuinely novel CDEs — once accepted, the boutique row is deprecated and the upstream row becomes canonical. Tracking promotion status quantifies DataTecnica's contribution to public CDE infrastructure.
